sections.label: Section 290
sections.original: καίτοι σ ’ ὁ φύσας χἠ τεκοῦσα προύδοσαν ,
sections.translation: Yet the father that begat thee , the mother that bare thee , gave thee up , though they had reached a time of life when to die were well , so saving thee their child , and winning noble death . For thou wert their only son , nor had they any hope , when thou wert dead , of other offspring .

sections.label: Section 295
sections.original: κἀγώ τ ’ ἂν ἔζων καὶ σὺ τὸν λοιπὸν χρόνον ,
sections.translation: And I should have lived and thou the remnant of our days , nor wouldst thou have wept thy wife ’ s loss , nor have had an orphan family . But some god hath caused these things to be even as they are . Enough ! Remember thou the gratitude due to me for this ;

sections.label: Section 300
sections.original: αἰτήσομαι γάρ σ ’— ἀξίαν μὲν οὔποτε ·
sections.translation: yea , for I shall never ask thee for an adequate return , for naught is prized more highly than our life ; but just is my request , as thou thyself must say , since thou no less than I dost love these children , if so be thou think ’ st aright . Be content to let them rule my house ,

sections.label: Section 305
sections.original: καὶ μὴ ʼπιγήμῃς τοῖσδε μητρυιὰν τέκνοις ,
sections.translation: and do not marry a new wife to be a stepmother to these children , for she from jealousy , if so she be a woman worse than me , will stretch out her hand against the children of our union . Then do not this , I do beseech thee . [*] For the stepmother that succeeds , hateth children

sections.label: Section 310
sections.original: τοῖς πρόσθʼ , ἐχίδνης οὐδὲν ἠπιωτέρα .
sections.translation: of a former match , cruel as the viper ’ s are her tender mercies . A son , ’ tis true , hath in his sire a tower of strength , to whom he speaks and has his answer back ; [*] but thou , my daughter , how shall thy maidenhood be passed in honour ? What shall thy experience be of thy father ’ s wife ?

sections.label: Section 315
sections.original: μή σοί τιν ’ αἰσχρὰν προσβαλοῦσα κληδόνα
sections.translation: She may fasten on thee some foul report in thy youthful bloom , and frustrate thy marriage . Never shall thy mother lead thee to the bridal bed , nor by her presence in thy travail hearten thee , my child , when a mother ’ s kindness triumphs over all .

sections.label: Section 320
sections.original: δεῖ γὰρ θανεῖν με · καὶ τόδ ’ οὐκ ἐς αὔριον
sections.translation: No , for I must die ; and lo ! this evil cometh to me not to-morrow nor yet on the third day of the month , but in a moment shall I be counted among the souls that are no more . Fare ye well , be happy ; and thou , husband , canst boast thou hadst a peerless wife ,

sections.label: Section 325
sections.original: ὑμῖν δέ , παῖδες , μητρὸς ἐκπεφυκέναι .
sections.translation: and you , children , that you had such an one for mother .

sections.label: Section 326
sections.original: θάρσει · πρὸ τούτου γὰρ λέγειν οὐχ ἅζομαι ·
sections.translation: Take heart ; I do not hesitate to answer for him ; he will perform all this , unless his mind should go astray .

sections.label: Section 328
sections.original: ἔσται τάδ ’ ἔσται , μὴ τρέσῃς · ἐπεὶ σ ’ ἐγὼ
sections.translation: It shall be so , fear not , it shall ; alive thou wert the only wife I had , and dead shalt thou ,

sections.label: Section 330
sections.original: μόνη κεκλήσῃ , κοὔτις ἀντὶ σοῦ ποτε
sections.translation: none else , be called mine ; no Thessalian maid shall ever take thy place and call me lord ; not though she spring from lineage high nor though besides she be the fairest of her sex . Of children I have enough ; god grant I may in them

sections.label: Section 335
sections.original: θεοῖς γενέσθαι · σοῦ γὰρ οὐκ ὠνήμεθα .
sections.translation: be blessed ! for in thee has it been otherwise . No year-long mourning will I keep for thee , but all my life through , lady : loathing the mother that bare me , and hating my father , for they were friends in word but not in deed .

sections.label: Section 340
sections.original: σὺ δ ’ ἀντιδοῦσα τῆς ἐμῆς τὰ φίλτατα
sections.translation: But thou didst give thy dearest for my life and save it . May I not then mourn to lose a wife like thee ? And I will put an end to revelry , to social gatherings o ’ er the wine , forego the festal crown and music which once reigned in my halls .

sections.label: Section 345
sections.original: οὐ γάρ ποτ ’ οὔτ ’ ἂν βαρβίτου θίγοιμ ’ ἔτι
sections.translation: For nevermore will I touch the lyre nor lift my soul in song to the Libyan flute , for thou hast taken with thee all my joy in life . But in my bed thy figure shall be laid full length , by cunning artists fashioned ;

sections.label: Section 350
sections.original: ᾧ προσπεσοῦμαι καὶ περιπτύσσων χέρας
sections.translation: thereon will I throw myself and , folding my arms about thee , call upon thy name , and think I hold my dear wife in my embrace , although I do not ; chill comfort this , no doubt , but still I shall relieve my soul of its sad weight ; and thou wilt come

sections.label: Section 355
sections.original: φοιτῶσά μ ’ εὐφραίνοις ἄν · ἡδὺ γὰρ φίλους
sections.translation:
to me in dreams and gladden me . For sweet it is to see our friends , come they when they will , e ’ en by night .
Had I the tongue , the tuneful voice of Orpheus to charm Demeter ’ s daughter or her husband by my lay and bring thee back from Hades ,

sections.label: Section 360
sections.original: κατῆλθον ἄν , καί μ ’ οὔθ ’ ὁ Πλούτωνος κύων
sections.translation: I had gone down , nor Pluto ’ s hound , nor Charon , ferryman of souls , whose hand is on the oar , had held me back , till to the light I had restored thee alive . At least do thou await me there , against the hour I die , prepare a home for me to be my true wife still .

sections.label: Section 365
sections.original: ἐν ταῖσιν αὐταῖς γάρ μ ’ ἐπισκήψω κέδροις
sections.translation: For in this same cedar coffin I will bid these children lay me with thee and stretch my limbs by thine ; for never even in death may I be severed from thee , alone found faithful of them all .

sections.label: Section 369
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἐγώ σοι πένθος ὡς φίλος φίλῳ
sections.translation: Lo ! I too will share with thee thy mourning for her , friend with friend ;

sections.label: Section 370
sections.original: λυπρὸν συνοίσω τῆσδε · καὶ γὰρ ἀξία .
sections.translation: for this is but her due .

sections.label: Section 371
sections.original: ὦ παῖδες , αὐτοὶ δὴ τάδ ’ εἰσηκούσατε
sections.translation: My children , ye with your own ears have heard your father ’ s promise , that he will never wed another wife to set her over you , nor e ’ er dishonour me .

sections.label: Section 374
sections.original: καὶ νῦν γέ φημι , καὶ τελευτήσω τάδε .
sections.translation: Yea , so I promise now , and accomplish it I will .

sections.label: Section 375
sections.original: ἐπὶ τοῖσδε παῖδας χειρὸς ἐξ ἐμῆς δέχου .
sections.translation: On these conditions receive the children from my hand .

sections.label: Section 376
sections.original: δέχομαι , φίλον γε δῶρον ἐκ φίλης χερός .
sections.translation: I receive them , dear pledges by a dear hapd given .

sections.label: Section 377
sections.original: σὺ νῦν γενοῦ τοῖσδ ’ ἀντ ’ ἐμοῦ μήτηρ τέκνοις .
sections.translation: Take thou my place and be a mother to these babes .

sections.label: Section 378
sections.original: πολλή μ ’ ἀνάγκη , σοῦ γ ’ ἀπεστερημένοις .
sections.translation: Sore will be their need when they are reft of thee .

sections.label: Section 379
sections.original: ὦ τέκνʼ , ὅτε ζῆν χρῆν μʼ , ἀπέρχομαι κάτω .
sections.translation: O my children , I am passing to that world below , when my life was needed most .

sections.label: Section 380
sections.original: οἴμοι , τί δράσω δῆτα σοῦ μονούμενος ;
sections.translation: Ah me , what can I do bereft of thee ?

sections.label: Section 381
sections.original: χρόνος μαλάξει σʼ · οὐδέν ἐσθ ’ ὁ κατθανών .
sections.translation: Thy sorrow Time will soothe ; ’ tis the dead who are as naught .

sections.label: Section 382
sections.original: ἄγου με σὺν σοί , πρὸς θεῶν , ἄγου κάτω .
sections.translation: Take me , O take me , I beseech , with thee ’ neath the earth .

sections.label: Section 383
sections.original: ἀρκοῦμεν ἡμεῖς οἱ προθνῄσκοντες σέθεν .
sections.translation: Enough that I in thy stead am dying .

sections.label: Section 384
sections.original: ὦ δαῖμον , οἵας συζύγου μ ’ ἀποστερεῖς .
sections.translation: O Destiny ! of what a wife art thou despoiling me !

sections.label: Section 385
sections.original: καὶ μὴν σκοτεινὸν ὄμμα μου βαρύνεται .
sections.translation: Lo ! the darkness deepens on my drooping eyes .

sections.label: Section 386
sections.original: ἀπωλόμην ἄρ ’, εἴ με δὴ λείψεις , γύναι .
sections.translation: Lost indeed am I , if thou , dear wife , wilt really leave me .

sections.label: Section 387
sections.original: ὡς οὐκέτ ’ οὖσαν οὐδὲν ἂν λέγοις ἐμέ .
sections.translation: Thou mayst speak of me as naught , as one whose life is o ’ er .

sections.label: Section 388
sections.original: ὄρθου πρόσωπον , μὴ λίπῃς παῖδας σέθεν .
sections.translation: Lift up thy face , leave not thy children .

sections.label: Section 389
sections.original: οὐ δῆθ ’ ἑκοῦσά γʼ , ἀλλὰ χαίρετʼ , ὦ τέκνα .
sections.translation: ’ Tis not my own free will ; O my babes , farewell !

sections.label: Section 390
sections.original: βλέψον πρὸς αὐτοὺς βλέψον .
sections.translation: Look , look on them but once .

sections.label: Section 390b
sections.original: οὐδέν εἰμ ’ ἔτι .
sections.translation: My end is come .

sections.label: Section 391
sections.original: τί δρᾷς ; προλείπεις ;
sections.translation: What mean ’ st thou ? art leaving us ?

sections.label: Section 391b
sections.original: χαῖρʼ .
sections.translation: Farewell !

sections.label: Section 391c
sections.original: ἀπωλόμην τάλας .
sections.translation: Lost ! lost ! woe is me !

sections.label: Section 392
sections.original: βέβηκεν , οὐκέτ ’ ἔστιν Ἀδμήτου γυνή .
sections.translation: She is gone , the wife of Admetus is no more .

sections.label: Section 1.393
sections.original: ἰώ μοι τύχας . μαῖα δὴ κάτω
sections.translation: O my hard fate ! My mother has passed to the realms below ; she lives no more ,

sections.label: Section 1.395
sections.original: πάτερ , ὑφ ’ ἁλίῳ .
sections.translation: dear father , ’ neath the sun . Alas for her ! she leaves us ere her time and to me bequeaths an orphan ’ s life . Behold that staring eye , those nerveless hands !

sections.label: Section 1.400
sections.original: ὑπάκουσον ἄκουσον , ὦ μᾶτερ , ἀντιάζω .
sections.translation: Hear me , mother , hear me , I implore ! ’ tis I who call thee now , I thy tender chick , printing my kisses on thy lips .

sections.label: Section 1.404
sections.original: τὴν οὐ κλύουσαν οὐδ ’ ὁρῶσαν · ὥστ ’ ἐγὼ
sections.translation: She cannot hear , she cannot see ;

sections.label: Section 1.405
sections.original: καὶ σφὼ βαρείᾳ συμφορᾷ πεπλήγμεθα .
sections.translation: a heavy blow hath fortune dealt us , you children and me .

sections.label: Section 1.406
sections.original: νέος ἐγώ , πάτερ , λείπομαι φίλας
sections.translation: O father , I am but a child to have my loving mother leave me here alone ; O cruel my fate , alas !

sections.label: Section 1.410
sections.original: σύ τέ μοι σύγκασι κούρα
sections.translation: and thine , my sister , sharer in my cup of woe . Woe to thee , father ! in vain , in vain didst thou take a wife and hast not reached the goal of eld with her ; for she is gone before , and now that thou art dead ,

sections.label: Section 1.415
sections.original: μᾶτερ , ὄλωλεν οἶκος .
sections.translation: my mother , our house is all undone .

sections.label: Section 1.416
sections.original: Ἄδμητʼ , ἀνάγκη τάσδε συμφορὰς φέρειν ·
sections.translation: Admetus , these misfortunes thou must bear . Thou art by no means the first nor yet shalt be the last of men to lose a wife of worth ; know this , we all of us are debtors unto death .

sections.label: Section 1.420
sections.original: ἐπίσταμαί γε , κοὐκ ἄφνω κακὸν τόδε
sections.translation: I understand ; this is no sudden flight of ill hither ; I was ware of it and long have pined . But since I am to . carry the dead forth to her burial , stay here with me and to that inexorable god in Hades raise your antiphone . While raise your antiphone .

sections.label: Section 1.425
sections.original: πᾶσιν δὲ Θεσσαλοῖσιν ὧν ἐγὼ κρατῶ
sections.translation: While to all Thessalians in my realm I do proclaim a general mourning for this lady , with hair shorn off and robes of sable hue ; all ye who harness steeds for cars , or single horses ride , cut off their manes with the sharp steel .

sections.label: Section 1.430
sections.original: αὐλῶν δὲ μὴ κατ ’ ἄστυ , μὴ λύρας κτύπος
sections.translation: Hush ’ d be every pipe , silent every lyre throughout the city till twelve full moons are past ; for never again shall I bury one whom I love more , no ! nor one more loyal to me ; honour from me is her due , for she for me hath died , she and she alone . [ Exeunt ADMETUS and EUMELUS , with the other children .

sections.label: Section 1.435
sections.original: ὦ Πελίου θύγατερ ,
sections.translation: Daughter of Pelias , be thine a happy life in that sunless home in Hades ’ halls ! Let Hades know , that swarthy god ,

sections.label: Section 1.440
sections.original: πηδαλίῳ τε γέρων
sections.translation: and that old man who sits to row and steer alike at his death-ferry , that he hath carried o ’ er the lake of Acheron in his two-oared skiff a woman peerless amidst her sex .

sections.label: Section 1.445
sections.original: πολλά σε μουσοπόλοι
sections.translation: Oft of thee the Muses ’ votaries shall sing on the seven-stringed mountain shell and in hymns that need no harp , [*] glorifying thee , oft as the season in his cycle cometh round at Sparta in that Carnean [*] month

sections.label: Section 1.450
sections.original: μηνός , ἀειρομένας
sections.translation: when all night long the moon sails high o ’ erhead , yea , and in splendid Athens , happy town . So glorious a theme has thy death bequeathed to tuneful bards .

sections.label: Section 2.455
sections.original: εἴθ ’ ἐπ ’ ἐμοὶ μὲν εἴη ,
sections.translation: Would it were in my power and range to bring thee to the light from the chambers of Hades and the streams of Cocytus with the oar that sweeps yon nether flood !
